Donald Trump gestures while seated in front of the fireplace in the Oval Office.

articles.title: One-way 'speedboats with explosives' launched by US for first time

children.content: The United States is using one-way drone boats in combat against Iran for the first time in its military history. A defence expert says America is catching on to this "cheaper and more effective" tactic employed by Iran and Ukraine.

image.altText: The Corsair drone boat appears to be turning in the sea.

articles.title: Trump ditches plan for 20 per cent toll on Strait of Hormuz shipping

children.content: Donald Trump reneges on his pledge to charge a 20 per cent fee on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a day after saying he would introduce it.

image.altText: Donald Trump sitting in a chair in the Oval Office with a stern expression.

articles.title: ASX rises after US reimposes naval blockade on Iran

children.content: The Australian share market is trading higher after some weaker-than-expected US inflation figures boosted Wall Street and reduced the likelihood of the Federal Reserve lifting interest rates. Follow live.

image.altText: ASX board viewed behind a person's head walking past window
Night cam vision of an explosion from above.

children.content: US President Donald Trump says he will not charge a 20 per cent toll on traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours before the US Navy's blockade is set to resume.

items.title: US, Iran exchange fire as Trump issues new threats and blockade begins

updates.title: Flow of Iran-linked vessels in the strait ticked up in hours before US blockade began
IRGC says it has 'destroyed' a US facility in Kuwait
US forces to leave Iraq after September 30

items.title: Australia's best-paid CEO makes 500 times the average salary

children.content: Australia's most highly paid executive collected a salary nearly 500 times that of the country's full-time average worker.

image.altText: Man with dark hair smiles at camera.

items.title: University of Melbourne defends management of antisemitism on campus

children.content: A royal commission has heard the University of Melbourne has been improving its processes for students to make complaints in the wake of antisemitic experiences by staff and students which escalated in 2024.

image.altText: Glyn Davis wearing a suit and tie sitting at a table with a small black microphone to his left

children.content: The PM is is outlining a national framework to deal with the emergence of artificial intelligence. Follow live.

items.title: Government to force data centres to minimise water usage, underwrite own power under new AI standards

updates.title: Climate Council says Labor must tie new data centres to new renewable energy
Greens say PM's AI speech 'missed the moment', won't protect Australians

items.title: Moira Deeming makes last-ditch plea to Liberal Party to retain candidacy

children.content: Victorian MP Moira Deeming faces being disendorsed as a Liberal candidate for refusing to apologise to former party leader Matthew Guy, after accusing him of assaulting her at a community event in May.

image.altText: Moira Deeming walking with a folder in her hand.

items.title: Disgraced footballer Nicky Winmar removed from AFL Hall of Fame

children.content: Winmar was found guilty earlier this month of three counts of unlawful assault and common law assault.

image.altText: Nicky wearing a grey coat and sunglasses.

items.title: Man shot dead in Sydney's north-west in 'very brazen attack'

children.content: A man has been shot dead in Sydney's north-west, with the homicide squad now leading the investigation.

image.altText: Three officers, police tape and a white car at the crime scene.

items.title: Sydney childcare abuse investigation expands to South Australia

children.content: A federal police investigation into an alleged childcare abuser has expanded to a remote Indigenous community in South Australia.

image.altText: Cloise up of man grinning in a green shirt, plant behind him

items.title: Timor-Leste scam centre compounds uncovered as police make 300 arrests

children.content: Police in Timor-Leste uncover a major international scam centre in the country, arresting more than 300 Chinese, Indonesian and Cambodian nationals in raids across the capital, Dili.

items.title: Woman with alleged links to Islamic State needs medical help, court told

children.content: A woman who was arrested over alleged links to the Islamic State terror group upon her arrival in Sydney has been denied bail for a second time, despite her lawyer saying she needed treatment for "significant psychological damage".

image.altText: Illustration of woman in white head covering and dark green top with beige background

items.title: Boy, 5, dies after being hit by 4WD in suburban Perth

children.content: A five-year-old boy dies and a 48-year-old woman is critically injured after being struck by a four-wheel drive outside a park in Cloverdale in Perth's south-east,

image.altText: Belgravia St sign on a pole on a sunny day.

items.title: Plumber takes down alleged car thief in viral video, but lawyer warns of risk

children.content: A Perth plumber has gone viral on social media after chasing down and restraining an alleged car thief after a botched getaway, but a prominent lawyer warns citizens' arrests are risky.

items.title: Father of murdered carer wins workplace injury claim

children.content: Tony Ticehurst has won his workplace compensation case against Peabody Energy, with the judge noting his murdered daughter Molly was helping care for him before her death.

image.altText: A man in a black t-shirt and sunglasses with his hand on his chin.

items.title: Truck driver's sacking for $100k ice cream bungle was fair, finds commission

children.content: The Fair Work Commission rules a trucking company was within its rights to fire a driver after he set the temperature of his trailer full of ice cream more than 20 degrees too warm.

image.altText: A road train with the logo EHR in green.

items.title: The bittersweet reality of becoming an empty-nester

children.content: Experts say there can be unexpected grief for parents that comes from children leaving home.

children.content: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

articles.title: France falters at first real hurdle as superstars fail to shine

children.content: France's 2026 World Cup campaign is ended by a poor 2-0 defeat to Spain, as the much-hyped collection of star forwards fail to turn up for the biggest game yet.

articles.title: Scottish traffic cone welcomed in Boston after World Cup friendship

children.content: The World Cup's obsession with Scotland fans and traffic cones continues as a witch's hat from Glasgow receives an official welcome from dignitaries in Boston.

image.altText: Massachusetts Governor Maura Healey poses wearing a traffic cone on her head next to two Scottish football fans.

articles.title: Spain soars into World Cup final with stunning upset over favourite France

children.content: Spain is through to the World Cup final thanks to a 2-0 win over France.

image.altText: Pedro Porro roars with delight

items.title: The new political parties hoping to deceive 'useful idiots' in Victoria

children.content: New political parties are seeking to exploit Victoria's anachronistic electoral system in the upper house to harvest voters' preferences and funnel them wherever they please.

image.altText: A Victorian Electoral Commission white-coloured ballot box

items.title: Why 10 years later stadiums are still a major bugbear of the AFLW

children.content: The AFL has introduced four double-headers this season, meaning games will be played at bigger venues, while the league remains steadfast on boutique grounds for standalone matches.

image.altText: The crowd watches on during the round five AFLW match between the Greater Western Sydney Giants and the Adelaide.

items.title: Children had to use toilet in front of others in police watchhouse, tribunal told

children.content: Advocates for young people say conditions in a Queensland watch house have not improved since three teenagers' human rights were breached. A tribunal heard the boys had limited privacy and had to use the toilet in front of others.

gallery.altText: Shots from the inside of a rundown, graffitied cell where mattresses lay on the floor.

thumbnail.altText: Shots from the inside of a rundown, graffitied cell where mattresses lay on the floor.

items.title: Man denied assisted dying wins partial victory in early test of ACT law

children.content: A tribunal has ruled on a man's bid to overturn his rejection from the ACT's assisted dying scheme in one of the first tests of how people can challenge such decisions.

image.altText: A building sign that reads 'ACT Civil and Administrative Tribunal'.

children.content: Millicent residents say they are concerned for the welfare of dozens of people sleeping rough in town.

items.title: Former ACT politician to stand trial on grooming charges

children.content: Former ACT Attorney-General Gordon Ramsay is committed to stand trial for allegedly grooming a teenage boy between 2022 and 2025.

image.altText: Gordon Ramsay walks outside the court in a suit and tie.

items.title: Comm Games 'might look a little different' for swim star after broken back

children.content: Mollie O'Callaghan reveals she has defied doctors' diagnoses to swim at the Commonwealth Games and Pan Pacific Championships despite spinal fractures and a shoulder injury.

image.altText: Mollie O'Callaghan in the water after the 200m freestyle.

items.title: Tourists turn away from NT as Kakadu travellers greeted by closure signs

children.content: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

gallery.altText: View of Twin Falls at Kakadu National Park.

thumbnail.altText: View of Twin Falls at Kakadu National Park.

items.title: Batteries now cheaper than gas for peak demand power supply

children.content: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

image.altText: Flames belch form two tall metal industrial chimneys.

items.title: Telstra slammed for putting 'burden of proof on consumers' for compensation

children.content: Telstra is assessing outage compensation claims, but consumer advocates say the process is cumbersome and puts the onus on customers rather than the telco at fault.

Tyrannosaurus rex
skeletons discovered makes history as the most valuable dinosaur fossil bought at auction.

items.title: Bali-based businessman's death leaves Australians chasing money

children.content: Cameron Hughes, 39, died in a Balinese immigration detention centre last week under yet-to-be-confirmed circumstances. The ABC can reveal the Perth businessman left behind hundreds of thousands of dollars in unfinished business, with dozens of customers potentially out of pocket.

image.altText: Cameron speaking while standing in front of a car in his workshop.

items.title: Japan ramps up defence spending to 'safeguard territory'

children.content: While the world has been watching the rise of China, Japan has been in the middle of its biggest defence shift since World War II.

image.altText: Japanese naval officers look over a ship.

items.title: The sledge that helped lighten Diamonds camp after brutal grand final

children.content: Kate Moloney has had to put her team's Super Netball grand final nightmare aside to focus on the Commonwealth Games. Some gentle ribbing from her Aussie teammate has helped her see the lighter side.

items.title: Writer E Jean Carroll collects $8m in Trump sex abuse and defamation case

children.content: Writer E Jean Carroll has collected nearly $US5.6 million ($8 million) from Donald Trump after a jury in 2023 found the US president liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.

image.altText: A woman stands in a street, wearing dark sunglasses, blue suit jacket, white shirt and blue and white scarf.

items.title: Child exploitation 'hiding in plain sight' in Thailand's sex industry

children.content: Thailand is struggling against a "hidden" world of child sexual exploitation that requires action from both the tourism industry and law enforcement agencies, according to NGOs.

image.altText: The black silhouette of a girl, with blurred lights in the background.
